sources.bibliography: Three Constructions of Covers, Journal of Combinatorial Mathematics and Combinatorial Computing 16 (1994), 17-25
sources.role: Primary 1994 source containing the uniqueness assertion
sources.scope_audit: Page 22 states that C(11,5,3)=20 and is unique, but gives no theorem, proof, hypotheses, or equivalence definition. Reference [1] for this assertion is W. H. Mills, Private communication.

sources.bibliography: The covering number C(11,5,3), Utilitas Mathematica 41 (1992), 63
sources.role: One-page primary note listed by the journal; full text not exposed by the maintained archive and not retrieved
sources.scope_audit: The archive establishes title, author, volume, year, and page 63 only. It cannot presently support a uniqueness theorem quotation.

sources.role: Maintained La Jolla Covering Repository representative
sources.scope_audit: Supplies a size-20 representative and bound entry, not an exhaustive classification proof.

verdict: No retrievable primary source located in this audit contains the requested theorem statement and proof of uniqueness. The 20-class computation is therefore exact but conditional on an unverified ordinary-cover uniqueness premise.
